name: Le Pic de Tarbesou - loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park
name_original: Le Pic de Tarbesou – boucle au départ du parking du Col de Pailhères
region_intro: Hiking around Ascou offers a diverse mountain environment in the French Pyrénées, with elevations typically between 1,500 and 2,000 meters. The region is characterized by majestic mountain passes like the Col de Pailhères and Col du Pradel, alongside numerous high-altitude lakes such as the Rabassoles lakes and Étang de Pailhères. Hikers can traverse through extensive beech forests and open mountain pastures, providing panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean peaks. Best hiking trails around Ascou The most popular hiking route is Le Pic de Tarbesou - loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the summit of Pic de Tarbésou, offering 360° panoramas and views of the Rabassoles lakes. Another top favourite among local hikers is La dent d’Orlu – hike starting from the parking de la Jasse de Cabane, a difficult 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This trail involves a significant elevation gain, providing a strenuous experience for seasoned hikers. Local hikers also love the Pic de Tarbesou – Étang de Pailhères loop from Port de Pailhères, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through mountain passes and past high-altitude lakes, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Ascou is defined by high mountain passes, serene high-altitude lakes, and expansive beech forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging alpine treks. The routes in Ascou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 2400 hikers have used komoot to explore Ascou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ascou?
A: Ascou offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 20 trails. These include 2 easy routes, 13 moderate trails, and 7 difficult paths, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ascou?
A: The hiking trails in Ascou are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning 360° panoramas from peaks like Pic de Tarbésou, the serene high-altitude lakes, and the expansive beech forests.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Ascou?
A: Hikers in Ascou can explore a variety of stunning natural features, including majestic mountain passes such as the &lt;strong&gt;Col de Pailhères&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Col du Pradel&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is also known for its beautiful high-altitude lakes like the three Rabassoles lakes, Étang de Pailhères, and Étang Noir de Rabassoles. You'll also traverse magnificent beech forests and expansive mountain pastures.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer incredible viewpoints. The summit of &lt;strong&gt;Pic de Tarbésou&lt;/strong&gt;, for example, provides a magnificent 360° panorama of the surrounding 2,000-meter peaks and views of the Rabassoles lakes. An easy hike also leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge du Chioula&lt;/a&gt;, which offers splendid Pyrenean vistas. Don't miss the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3456427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mérens sulphur springs&lt;/a&gt; for a natural attraction.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ascou?
A: Yes, Ascou features several excellent circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949865860/le-pic-de-tarbesou-loop-starting-from-the-col-de-pailheres-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Pic de Tarbesou - loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1476427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Tarbesou – Étang de Pailhères loop from Port de Pailhères&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.7-mile (9.2 km) route that takes you past high-altitude lakes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Ascou?
A: The best time to hike in Ascou is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is milder and the high-altitude trails are clear of snow. During these months, you can fully enjoy the mountain pastures, lakes, and panoramic views. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Ascou?
A: Ascou offers a range of trails, including some suitable for families. While specific 'easy' family routes aren't detailed, the region's diverse network includes options that are less strenuous. For example, the easy hike to the Refuge du Chioula provides a rewarding panorama without extreme difficulty. Always check the trail's distance and elevation gain to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Ascou?
A: Many trails in the French Pyrénées, including those around Ascou, are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to mountain environments. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Ascou?
A: Many popular trailheads in Ascou, such as the starting point for the Pic de Tarbésou loop, have designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949865860/le-pic-de-tarbesou-loop-starting-from-the-col-de-pailheres-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Tarbesou loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from the Col de Pailhères car park. Similarly, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949828645/la-dent-dorlu-hike-starting-from-the-parking-de-la-jasse-de-cabane" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La dent d’Orlu hike&lt;/a&gt; begins from the parking de la Jasse de Cabane. Look for specific parking information at the start of your chosen route.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Ascou?
A: Yes, Ascou provides several challenging hikes for seasoned adventurers. One notable difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949828645/la-dent-dorlu-hike-starting-from-the-parking-de-la-jasse-de-cabane" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La dent d’Orlu – hike starting from the parking de la Jasse de Cabane&lt;/a&gt;, which involves a significant elevation gain over its 2.9 miles (4.6 km). The region has 7 trails classified as difficult, offering strenuous experiences and rewarding views for those with good fitness and experience.

Q: What amenities are available for hikers in Ascou and nearby?
A: While Ascou itself is a small village, the nearby town of Ax-les-Thermes serves as a hub for sports and nature lovers. Here you can find various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. For a unique natural experience, the sulphurous hot springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3456427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les sources sulfureuses de Mérens&lt;/a&gt; are ideal for relaxation before or after a hike.
tips: The setting here is truly stunning. The Rabassoles ponds shine in the valley, standing out against the surrounding granite cliffs. You can see the Rabassoles pond (1,851 metres), the Blue pond (1,920 metres) and the Black pond (1,970 metres).
The surroundings of the Etang Noir offer lovely views of the Rabassoles pond.
The ascent of the Tarbésou peak offers magnificent views of the Rabassoles ponds.
The peak of Tarbésou, which culminates at 2,364 meters and overlooks the ponds of Rabassoles, offers a magnificent belvedere.

waypoints.name: View of the Étang de Rabassoles
